mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% in March 2010 reflects post-crisis labor slack
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume indicates accelerating consumer demand and inventory replenishment cycles, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 60-day forward window.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363194M in March 2010 reflects post-recession demand recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 60d SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rate combined with rising inflation expectations signals monetary accommodation that reduces real discount rates, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive equity returns over 5 trading days as market reprices duration exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 217.729 in March 2010
regime_hint: monetary easing with emerging inflation in post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_5d on FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential demand contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth rate falls below -0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.61 in March 2010 reflects post-recession supply recovery
regime_hint: supply-side contraction or demand-driven inventory correction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative INDPRO signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer income and spending, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days through earnings growth channel.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15% AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129750K and UNRATE at 9.7% in March 2010
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase with synchronized employment and unemployment improvement
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals weakening construction demand and reduced future consumer wealth effects from housing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 575K units in February 2010 reflects post-crisis housing weakness
regime_hint: housing market contraction or demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low inflation measured by CPI combined with accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) signals central bank commitment to easy conditions, reducing equity risk premium and producing positive returns over 5 trading days as investors extend duration.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.729 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% in March 2010
regime_hint: any regime with low inflation and accommodative policy
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation between (FEDFUNDS + CPIAUCSL trend) and 5d SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 combined with rising retail sales signals sustained demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13149.5B and RSAFS at 363194M in Q4 2009 / March 2010
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with synchronized GDP and retail growth
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite both signals active.
